---
title: "urban"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-10"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

```

```{r}
urban_2 <- read.csv("urban.csv", fileEncoding='latin1',check.names=F)

head(urban_2)
```

```{r}
summary(urban_2)

```


```{r}
urban_2$year <- as.factor(urban_2$year)
urban_2$high.school <-as.factor(urban_2$high.school)

```


```{r}
#does social engagement differ between students with an urban hs background (yes/no)?

t_test_social <- t.test(social.total ~ high.school, data = urban_2)
summary(t_test_social)

t_test_academic <- t.test(academic.total ~ high.school, data = urban_2)
summary(t_test_academic)


```

```{r}
year_social <- aov(social.total ~ year, data = urban_2)
summary(year_social)

year_academic <- aov(academic.total ~ year, data = urban_2)
summary(year_academic)

```

```{r}
cor.test(
  x = urban_2$academic.total,
  y = urban_2$social.total, 
  method = "pearson"
)

```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)

# Scatter plot with regression line and correlation info
ggplot(urban_2, aes(x = academic.total, y = social.total)) +
  geom_point(color = "#2C7BB6", alpha = 0.7, size = 3) +  # scatter points
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "#D7191C", linetype = "dashed") +  # regression line
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", 
           aes(label = paste(..r.label.., ..p.label.., sep = "~`,`~")), 
           label.x = min(urban_2$academic.total), 
           label.y = max(urban_2$social.total), 
           size = 5) +  # add correlation and p-value
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Between Academic and Social Engagement",
    x = "Academic Engagement (Total Score)",
    y = "Social Engagement (Total Score)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

```
